1960.note: Iconic drummer Tony Meehan, born in Hampstead, Camden, provided the driving beat behind this massive UK number one hit.

1970.note: Guitar virtuoso Paul Kossoff, born in Hampstead, Camden, co-wrote and played the unmistakable riff for this classic anthem.

1971.note: Born and raised above his family’s shop on Archway Road in the borough, Stewart achieved global superstardom with this breakout track.
Legendary drummer Charlie Watts was born in Bloomsbury, Camden, providing the effortless swing that defined the band's golden era.

1975.note: Produced by Camden-born Roy Thomas Baker, whose multi-layered production techniques brought the operatic masterpiece to life.

1977.note: Marley lived at 34 Ridgmount Gardens in Camden while writing and recording major parts of this monumental album.
Frontman and guitarist Hugh Cornwell was born in Camden, anchoring the band's gritty, melodic New Wave breakthrough.

1979.note: Camden-born drummer Hugo Burnham supplied the jagged, danceable rhythms central to this foundational post-punk anthem.

1980.note: The deafening trio were a staple at the Camden Roundhouse, which served as a home turf for their legendary, high-octane gigs.
Formed and led by Camden-born frontman and brilliant songwriter Adrian Borland, a pioneer of the underground indie movement.

1983.note: Drummer John Keeble, born in Camden, provided the steady soul-pop groove for the band's most internationally recognized ballad.

1987.note: Iconic lead guitarist Slash (Saul Hudson) was born in Hampstead, Camden, before later relocating to Los Angeles.

1994.note: The quintessential 90s indie group famously signed their record contract on the pool table inside Camden's Good Mixer pub.

1995.note: The Madness frontman paid direct homage to his roots with this solo single celebrating the atmosphere of the borough's markets.
Formed directly out of the indie crowd at The Good Mixer, they quickly became the ultimate poster boys for the Camden Britpop era.

2014.note: Fronted by Camden-born Ellie Rowsell, the alternative group rose to fame with early shows around North London before winning the Mercury Prize.

2017.note: Born in Camden, the singer-songwriter crafted this baroque pop ballad which went viral globally as a modern indie classic.

2020.note: The Camden-born saxophonist and composer stands at the forefront of the vibrant, modern London jazz renaissance.

2023.note: Born and raised in Camden, the musician and producer crafts rich, genre-bending soundscapes capturing contemporary British identity.
